prompt: This method is supposed to double every element in the array, but the array is unchanged after the call. What is the bug?
choices: for-each loops cannot be used with int arrays
The for-each loop variable 'n' is a copy of each element — assigning to it does not modify the array; use an indexed loop with arr[i] *= 2
int arrays are passed by value so no method can modify them
n *= 2 should be written as n = n * 2 for the assignment to take effect
explanation: In a for-each loop over a primitive array, the loop variable is a copy of the element. Modifying 'n' has no effect on the array. Fix: for (int i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) { arr[i] *= 2; }
